description: Figure 1 comprises three panels presenting the thermomechanical processing design, equilibrium phase predictions, and thermal dilation verification for an Fe-8Mn-5Al-0.2C (wt.%) low-density medium Mn steel. Panel (a) is a schematic process route showing: homogenization at 1100°C for 2 h, hot forging with air cooling, hot rolling with soaking at 1100°C for 2 h and two passes (50% reduction each, intermediate reheating at 1100°C for 5 min), water quenching, and intercritical annealing at 750°C for 5–180 min followed by air cooling. Panel (b) displays the Thermo-Calc equilibrium phase fraction versus temperature plot (TCFE9 database), indicating that BCC_A2 (δ-ferrite) persists from solidification to room temperature and coexists with FCC_A1 (austenite) in a duplex region from ~650°C to ~1350°C; this underpins the selection of 750°C for intercritical annealing. Panel (c) presents dilatometry heating-cooling curves for the hot-forged sample under 5 min and 15 min holding times, both nearly overlapping with only a slight deviation around ~600°C (marked by a black ellipse), which caption states reflects carbide/cementite dissolution rather than massive ferrite-to-austenite transformation, confirming the stability of δ-ferrite and minimal phase transformation during thermal cycling.
